title: alnus glabrata subsp. durangensis
intro: Alnus glabrata subsp. durangensis, also known as Durango alder, is a subspecies of the alder tree native to the mountainous regions of Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Durango alder include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support the immune system, making it a valuable resource for herbal medicine. Durango alder can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies to treat various ailments.
